shelf_life_fridge: Up to 2 weeks (skin darkens but fruit stays good)
storage_tips: Store at room temperature. Separate from other fruits to slow ripening. To speed ripening, place in a paper bag.
freshness_signs: Bright yellow with minimal brown spots = peak freshness. Green = unripe. Mostly brown = overripe but great for baking.
spoilage_signs: Mushy texture, fermented smell, mold on stem, completely black skin with liquid seeping
fun_fact: Bananas are technically berries, while strawberries are not!

storage_tips: Ripen at room temperature. Once ripe (yields to gentle pressure), refrigerate. Sprinkle cut avocado with lemon juice to prevent browning.
freshness_signs: Dark green to black skin, yields slightly to pressure = ripe. Bright green and hard = unripe.
spoilage_signs: Very mushy, dark brown/black flesh throughout, sour or off smell, visible mold
fun_fact: Avocados don't ripen on the tree — they only start ripening after being picked!

storage_tips: Wrap in paper towels and store in a plastic bag in the crisper drawer. Don't wash until ready to use.
freshness_signs: Crisp leaves, vibrant green color, no wilting
spoilage_signs: Slimy leaves, brown or black spots, foul smell, excessive wilting
fun_fact: Lettuce is a member of the daisy family!

storage_tips: Store stem-side down at room temperature for best flavor. Only refrigerate if very ripe and you need to slow spoilage.
freshness_signs: Firm but yields slightly to pressure, vibrant red color, pleasant smell at stem
spoilage_signs: Very soft, wrinkled skin, mold, leaking fluid, off smell
fun_fact: Tomatoes are the most popular garden vegetable (technically fruit) in the US!

storage_tips: Don't wash until ready to eat. Store in a single layer on paper towels in the fridge. Remove any moldy ones immediately.
freshness_signs: Bright red color all over, green leafy caps, firm texture, sweet smell
spoilage_signs: White or gray fuzzy mold, mushy spots, dull color, fermented smell
fun_fact: Strawberries are the only fruit with seeds on the outside — about 200 per berry!

storage_tips: Refrigerate for longest life. Keep away from other produce — apples release ethylene gas that ripens other fruits faster.
freshness_signs: Firm when squeezed, smooth skin, no bruises
spoilage_signs: Soft spots, wrinkled skin, mealy texture, brown discoloration throughout, mold
fun_fact: There are over 7,500 varieties of apples grown worldwide!

storage_tips: Remove green tops before storing (they draw moisture). Store in a plastic bag in the crisper. Can store in water for extra crispness.
freshness_signs: Firm, bright orange, smooth surface, no cracks
spoilage_signs: Rubbery/bendy texture, slimy surface, white film, dark spots, foul smell
fun_fact: Carrots were originally purple! Orange carrots were developed in the Netherlands in the 17th century.

storage_tips: Store unwashed in a loose plastic bag in the crisper. Don't seal tightly — it needs airflow.
freshness_signs: Tight, dark green florets, firm stalks, no yellowing
spoilage_signs: Yellow florets, limp stalks, slimy texture, strong unpleasant odor
fun_fact: Broccoli contains more protein per calorie than steak!
